claim_verdicts.reason: Evidence directly states "We used the Adam optimizer" in the Optimizer section.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The optimizer used in Attention Is All You Need is the Adam optimizer.
The exact beta1 value specified for the optimizer is 0.9.
The exact beta2 value specified for the optimizer is 0.98.
The exact epsilon value specified for the optimizer is 10^{-9}.

claim_verdicts.reason: Both passages together directly support: "increasing the learning rate linearly for the first warmup_steps training steps, and decreasing it thereafter proportionally to the inverse square root of the step number. We used warmup_steps = 4000."
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The learning rate is increased linearly for the first warmup_steps training steps and decreased thereafter proportionally to the inverse square root of the step number, with warmup_steps = 4000.

title: Extract the exact optimizer name and its hyperparameters (beta1, beta2, epsilon) as used in the Attention Is All You Need paper, for inclusion in a strict reproduction checklist (optimizer portion only). Do not substitute generic Adam defaults; quote values verbatim from the paper.
